qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unbounded Accept-header wildcard expansion in Mime::Type.parse enables algorithmic-efficiency DoS
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different vulnerable component and root cause: the finding concerns ActionPack Mime::Type.parse wildcard expansion in the HTTP Accept parser, while the advisory concerns Active Storage DiskService#path_for failing to keep blob-key paths inside the storage root.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described Active Storage DiskService#path_for path traversal root cause. The only qualifying finding is an unrelated ActionPack Accept-header parser algorithmic-efficiency denial of service.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-9xrj-h377-fr87 describes Active Storage DiskService#path_for not validating that a resolved blob-key filesystem path stays within the storage root, enabling path traversal read/write/delete.

accept_header_wildcard_amplification_dos/vulnerability_report.json identifies Mime::Type.parse and MimeNegotiation#accepts processing repeated Accept-header wildcard entries, causing CPU and allocation amplification.
The qualifying finding's code locations are actionpack/lib/action_dispatch/http/mime_type.rb and mime_negotiation.rb, not Active Storage DiskService path handling.
blockers_to_higher_tier: No advisory-matched qualifying finding was present, so advisory-specific PoC execution was not applicable.
